title: AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge 2026 Opens Applications for Young African Healthcare Innovators
summary: The African Institute for Mathematical Sciences (AIMS), with the support of the William Flora Hewlett Foundation, has officially launched applications for the 2026 AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge — a major opportunity designed to empower young African innovators who are creating impactful healthcare solutions aligned with the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Following the success and lessons learned from the 2025 edition, the 2026 challenge aims to identify, support, and scale innovative, youth-led projects that can improve healthcare delivery, strengthen health systems, and tackle pressing healthcare challenges across Africa through technology and innovation. The competition is specifically targeted at passionate STEM graduates under the age of 35, with a strong encouragement for women innovators to apply. Through this initiative, AIMS seeks to support the next generation of African problem-solvers who are using digital technology and data-driven innovation to improve healthcare outcomes across the continent. Selected participants will receive the prestigious Innovation Fellowship Award, which includes funding, mentorship, technical support, entrepreneurship training, and access to the AIMS Rwanda Innovation Program. About the AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge 2026 The AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge is a platform that empowers young African innovators to develop scalable and sustainable healthcare solutions using technology, data, and entrepreneurship. The challenge focuses on: Advancing healthcare innovation in Africa Supporting SDG-focused technological solutions Empowering youth-led startups and initiatives Strengthening healthcare systems through digital innovation Creating opportunities for collaboration, mentorship, and business growth Participants will engage in a data-focused hackathon designed to refine and strengthen their proposed healthcare innovations. The hackathon will provide a collaborative environment where innovators can transform ideas into practical, scalable solutions capable of driving real-world impact. Who Can Apply? Applications are open to: African STEM graduates Young innovators under the age of 35 Individuals passionate about healthcare innovation Applicants with digital technology-driven healthcare solutions Early-stage innovators and entrepreneurs Women innovators are strongly encouraged to apply Applicants should demonstrate creativity, innovation, leadership potential, and a strong commitment to solving healthcare challenges in Africa. Benefits of the AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge 2026 Winners selected for the Innovation Fellowship Award will gain access to an extensive support package aimed at helping them develop and scale their solutions. Benefits include: 1. Four-Month Innovation Fellowship Stipend Selected fellows will receive financial support through a 4-month innovation fellowship stipend, enabling them to focus on developing and improving their healthcare solution. 2. Participation in the AIMS Rwanda Innovation Program Winners will have the opportunity to join the AIMS Rwanda Innovation Program, where they will gain access to innovation resources, collaborative opportunities, and entrepreneurial support. 3. Mentorship from Global Experts Participants will receive mentorship from international entrepreneurship development experts and professionals in healthcare innovation, technology, and business strategy. 4. Technical and Business Development Training Fellows will undergo specialized training sessions covering: Product development Healthcare innovation Business strategy Startup growth Data and technology application Entrepreneurship development 5. Networking Opportunities Participants will connect with: Industry experts Development agencies Innovation ecosystem leaders Potential collaborators and investors 6. Opportunity to Showcase and Pilot Solutions Winning innovations may be showcased and piloted within the SDG innovation ecosystem, helping participants gain visibility and potentially scale their impact across Africa. SDG Innovation Challenge Hackathon A key part of the program is the SDG Innovation Challenge Hackathon, where participants will: Refine and strengthen their proposed solutions Collaborate with fellow innovators Apply digital technology to healthcare challenges Develop practical and scalable healthcare innovations Receive expert feedback and support The hackathon is designed to transform innovative concepts into actionable healthcare solutions capable of delivering measurable social impact. Why This Opportunity Matters Africa continues to face numerous healthcare challenges, ranging from limited access to quality healthcare services to shortages in healthcare infrastructure and digital healthcare solutions. The AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge 2026 provides a powerful platform for young innovators to: Address urgent healthcare problems Build impactful technology solutions Gain international exposure Access mentorship and funding Join a growing innovation ecosystem focused on sustainable development This initiative also contributes to achieving the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals by encouraging innovative approaches to healthcare improvement across the continent. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge 2026 is: 1 June 2026 (11:59 PM GMT+2) Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their applications before the deadline. Learn more and apply Interested students can learn more about the AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge 2026 and apply HERE . Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post AIMS SDG Innovation Challenge 2026 Opens Applications for Young African Healthcare Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNDP Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Opportunities 2026: Procurement Analyst (Bangkok) and Policy Analyst (Geneva)
summary: The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) has opened two prestigious Junior Professional Officer (JPO) positions for 2026, exclusively sponsored by the Government of the Republic of Korea. These roles—Procurement Analyst (Bangkok, Thailand) and Policy Analyst for Effective Development Cooperation (Geneva, Switzerland)—offer young Korean professionals a unique pathway into the multilateral development sector. According to the official posting, “This post is opened in the context of the Junior Professional Officer (JPO) scheme sponsored by the Government of the Republic of Korea and is addressed exclusively to nationals of the Republic of Korea.” These positions are designed to build technical expertise, leadership capacity, and global development experience while contributing directly to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). Both roles are full‑time, P2‑level fixed‑term appointments with an initial one‑year contract and the possibility of extension. About the UNDP JPO Programme The UNDP JPO Programme equips emerging leaders with hands‑on experience in development cooperation, policy implementation, and international operations. As stated in the job description, the programme “equips outstanding young leaders with the skills and experience required to advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) and make a positive difference in the world.” Key features include: Structured supervision and mentorship A formal work plan with measurable results Annual performance reviews Access to masterclasses, induction courses, and training Networking with global UNDP staff Duty‑related travel and training allocations (DTTA) Opportunities for long‑term career development within the UN system JPO POSITION 1: Procurement Analyst, Operations Effectiveness Compliance (Bangkok, Thailand) Organizational Context The role is situated within the Regional Bureau for Asia and the Pacific (RBAP), supporting 36 countries and territories. The Procurement Analyst will work with the Operational Effectiveness Compliance (OE C) team, contributing to oversight, quality assurance, procurement compliance, and operational efficiency across the region. The document notes that the role “is designed to progressively build independent procurement advisory capacity.” Key Duties and Responsibilities 1. Implementation of Procurement Strategies Ensure full compliance with UNDP procurement rules and internal controls Support monitoring of procurement processes across country offices Organize RFQs, ITBs, RFPs, and evaluation processes Provide on‑site and remote procurement support Translate corporate procurement policies into country‑level SOPs 2. Development of Regional Framework Agreements Analyze cost‑saving opportunities Develop and manage supplier rosters 3. Support Procurement Strategy Development Coordinate sourcing strategies and contract management Promote ERP e‑tendering modules 4. Procurement Process Guidance Advise on procurement planning and bottleneck analysis Review bid documents and evaluation criteria Support procurement committees and transactional requests 5. Knowledge Building Organize regional procurement training Train staff on e‑tendering and automated processes Document lessons learned and best practices Education and Experience Requirements Master’s degree + 2 years experience OR Bachelor’s degree + 4 years experience Fields: Procurement, Logistics, Business Administration, Public Administration, Economics CIPS Level 3 highly desirable Experience with ERP systems (Oracle/Quantum) preferred Prior UN or international organization experience is an advantage JPO POSITION 2: Policy Analyst, Effective Development Cooperation (Geneva, Switzerland) Organizational Context This role supports the OECD‑UNDP Joint Support Team for the Global Partnership for Effective Development Co‑operation (GPEDC). The GPEDC is a major global platform established under the Busan Partnership Agreement to strengthen development effectiveness worldwide. The job description states that the initiative aims “to maximize the effectiveness of all forms of co-operation for development for the shared benefits of people, planet, prosperity and peace.” Key Duties and Responsibilities 1. Support the 5th GPEDC Monitoring Round Assist in rolling out the global monitoring exercise Compile country‑level policy documents Draft country‑led stories and communication materials Contribute to policy briefs and global reports 2. Knowledge Sharing and Advocacy Draft concept notes, speaker bios, and communication materials Support website updates and social media content Assist partner governments on the GPEDC Steering Committee Produce stories of progress and event summaries 3. Support to Global Effectiveness Events Assist in preparations for High‑Level Meetings and the Busan Forum Manage participant lists, invitations, and logistics Coordinate with host governments and donors Education and Experience Requirements Master’s degree + 2 years experience OR Bachelor’s degree + 4 years experience Fields: Public Policy, Politics, Economics, Development Studies Experience with large datasets, development cooperation, and policy analysis required Experience with UN, multilateral bodies, or multi‑stakeholder initiatives is an advantage General Eligibility Requirements for Both Positions Language Requirements Fluency in Korean and English French or Spanish desirable Valid English test scores: TOEFL iBT 100 TEPS 430 IELTS 7 TOEIC 900 Mandatory Attachments Motivation letter Resident Register (English) Degree certificates English test certificate Employment certificates (if shortlisted) Military service certificate (if applicable) Low‑income or disability certificates (if applicable) Deadline for both positions: 26 June 2026, 09:59 Procurement Analyst, Operations Effectiveness and Compliance (OE C) Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here. Policy Analyst, Effective Development Cooperation Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more job opportunities here The post UNDP Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Opportunities 2026: Procurement Analyst (Bangkok) and Policy Analyst (Geneva) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Future Leaders Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity for Africa’s Emerging Air Quality Researchers
summary: The Future Leaders Fellowship 2026 , jointly organized by the Africa Clean Air Network (AfriCAN) and the Health Effects Institute (HEI) , is a prestigious part‑time, virtual fellowship designed to empower the next generation of African researchers in the fields of air pollution, environmental health, air quality monitoring, modelling, and policy engagement . This six‑month program (September 2026 – March 2027) offers graduate students across Africa a rare chance to deepen their expertise, collaborate with leading scientists, and contribute to impactful research that shapes the continent’s environmental future. Program Overview The fellowship provides a structured, mentorship‑driven research experience. Selected fellows will be paired with expert mentors from top academic and research institutions across Africa and beyond. Each fellow will work on a tailored research project , gaining hands‑on experience, technical skills, and professional exposure. Key components of the program include: One‑on‑one mentorship with established researchers Participation in professional development seminars Collaboration with the Africa Clean Air Network Secretariat Engagement with a broader network of environmental and public health experts Presentation of final research outcomes during a public webinar Opportunities to produce peer‑reviewed publications, policy briefs, or blogs Fellows must have access to a computer and be able to attend regular virtual meetings throughout the program. Fellowship Benefits The Future Leaders Fellowship offers meaningful support to ensure fellows can fully engage in their research: USD 1,800 fellowship award , paid directly to each fellow Travel support to attend the Africa Clean Air Forum 2027 Access to a continental network of researchers, policymakers, and environmental advocates Skill‑building through seminars, mentorship, and collaborative research Visibility through public presentations and potential publications Mentor‑Led Research Opportunities Applicants may select from the following research themes, each guided by a distinguished mentor: Assessment of available emissions inventories for key air pollution sources in Africa Mentor: Dr. Evelyne Toure, Université Félix Houphouët Boigny, Côte d Ivoire Rapid review of peer‑reviewed literature on air pollution and heat in Africa Mentor: Dr. Adelaide Lusambili, NextGen for Earth, Kenya Mapping and assessment of air quality monitoring across Africa Mentors: Dr. Deo Okure (AirQo, Uganda) and Dr. Allison Hughes (Afri‑SET, Ghana) Assessment of long‑term air quality trends in African cities Mentor: Dr. Pallavi Pant, Health Effects Institute, USA Analysis of public perception of air pollution in Africa using news and social media Mentor: Dr. Melina Platas, New York University, Abu Dhabi Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements: Must be enrolled in a graduate program (Master’s or PhD) at an institution based in Africa Master’s students must have completed at least one full year of study by September 2026 Research interests must align with one of the listed fellowship topics Applicants must not be postdoctoral researchers or faculty members Must demonstrate strong interest in air quality, environmental health, or related fields Evaluation Process Applications will be reviewed by the HEI and AfriCAN program committee. Selection will be based on: Quality and clarity of the application Relevance of the fellowship to the applicant’s academic and research goals Potential contribution to environmental science and air quality research Key Dates Applications open: May 18, 2026 Applications close: June 12, 2026 at 11:59 GMT Review of applications: July 2026 Selection of fellows: August 2026 Program start date: September 1, 2026 Program end date: March 2027 Program Sponsor This fellowship is generously supported by the KRNmosaic Charitable Foundation , whose mission centers on improving global health and education. Suggested Follow‑Up Actions Explore the research themes Learn about mentor backgrounds Review application tips For more information about this opportunity and to apply visit here. Discover more fellowship opportunities here. The post Future Leaders Fellowship 2026: A Transformative Opportunity for Africa’s Emerging Air Quality Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants 2026: Up to £200,000 Available for SRHR Advocacy Projects in Eligible Countries
summary: Civil society organisations working to advance sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR) now have an exciting opportunity to secure major funding through the 2026 AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants programme. Applications are officially open from 21 May to 16 June 2026 , offering grants between £100,000 and £200,000 for projects lasting 24 to 36 months . This funding opportunity is designed to support small to medium-sized civil society organisations seeking to strengthen their advocacy work, expand organisational capacity, and drive sustainable change in SRHR policies, services, and social norms. What Are the AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants? The AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants support advocacy initiatives focused on improving access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. A unique feature of this funding is that up to 40% of the grant can be used for organisational development and strengthening institutional effectiveness . The programme aims to empower organisations that are working to influence policies, improve services, reduce stigma, and promote inclusive SRHR systems, particularly for marginalised and underserved populations. Funding Details Grant Amount £100,000 – £200,000 Project Duration 2 to 3 years (24–36 months) Application Window Opens: 21 May 2026 Deadline: 16 June 2026 by 17:00 UK Time Registration Deadline Applicants must register on the Grants Portal by: 5 June 2026 at 12:00 UK Time Due to high demand, organisations are encouraged to register early to avoid delays. Key Priority Areas AmplifyChange is looking to fund projects that align with one or more of its core SRHR priority themes: Violence Abortion Inclusion SRHR Education Access to SRH Services Projects can also address intersections between multiple themes. In response to global funding trends, at least 30% of available funding will specifically support applications focused on: Governments ensuring funding for SRHR Recognising that some legally marginalised groups may face challenges engaging directly in this area, organisations are also encouraged to submit proposals under other relevant outcome areas aligned with their expertise and operating context. Outcome Areas Applications should contribute to one or more of the following outcomes: Increased awareness of SRHR as human rights Policy and legal reforms improving SRHR access Improved access to quality sexual and reproductive health services More inclusive social norms and reduced stigma and discrimination Governments ensuring sustainable funding for SRHR Cross-Cutting Themes AmplifyChange also welcomes projects integrating broader issues connected to SRHR and wellbeing, including: Climate Justice Mental Health Pleasure and Bodily Autonomy Who Can Apply? To be eligible, organisations must: Be registered and implementing projects in an eligible country Have a functioning organisational bank account Be a small to medium-sized organisation Have an average annual expenditure between £150,000 and £350,000 over the past two years Provide financial evidence such as audited reports or profit and loss statements Only one application per organisation will be accepted during this funding round. Eligible Countries Projects must be implemented in eligible countries across Africa and Asia, including: Angola, Bangladesh, Benin, Bhutan, Botswana, Burkina Faso, Burundi, Cameroon, Cape Verde, Central African Republic, Chad, Comoros, Côte d’Ivoire, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Djibouti, Equatorial Guinea, Eritrea, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Gabon, The Gambia, Ghana, Guinea, Guinea-Bissau, India, Kenya, Lesotho, Liberia, Madagascar, Malawi, Maldives, Mali, Mauritania, Mauritius, Mozambique, Myanmar, Namibia, Nepal, Niger, Nigeria, Pakistan, Republic of Congo, Rwanda, São Tomé and Príncipe, Senegal, Sierra Leone, Somalia, South Africa, South Sudan, Sri Lanka, Sudan, Tanzania, Togo, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe. What Type of Projects Are Encouraged? AmplifyChange is particularly interested in projects that: Strengthen organisational capacity and sustainability Advocate for increased government funding for SRHR Address discriminatory social norms and unequal power dynamics Scale successful advocacy models Support innovative approaches with proven early success Empower marginalised communities to claim their rights Influence laws, policies, and implementation systems Build coalitions and collective action for long-term change Focus on Marginalised Communities The fund strongly prioritises organisations working with populations facing restricted access to SRHR, including: Young people Refugees People with disabilities Rural populations Urban poor communities Ethnic minorities Sexual minorities Webinar Opportunities for Applicants Interested applicants can join free webinars to better understand the application process and requirements. Webinar Dates About the Grant: 21 May 2026 at 10:00 UK Time Monitoring Evaluation Requirements: 26 May 2026 at 10:00 UK Time Why This Opportunity Matters As global conversations around gender equality, reproductive justice, youth empowerment, and inclusive healthcare continue to evolve, funding opportunities like the AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants provide critical support for grassroots and civil society organisations leading transformative advocacy efforts. For organisations committed to advancing SRHR and strengthening community-driven change, this funding round presents an important opportunity to scale impact, strengthen systems, and contribute to lasting policy and social transformation. How to Apply Interested organisations should begin preparing early, review the eligibility criteria carefully, and complete Grants Portal registration before the registration deadline. Apply for the AmplifyChange Strengthening Grant Are You an Existing User? Login Here: https://amplifychange.fluxx.io/portal Are You a New Applicant? Register Here: https://amplifychange.fluxx.io/user_sessions/new?lang=en FAQs If you are unsure whether your organisation meets AmplifyChange’s eligibility criteria, be sure to review the Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs) for detailed clarification on eligibility, funding requirements, and the application process. Read More: https://amplifychange.org/faqs/ Application Guidance Interested applicants are strongly encouraged to review the official application guidance to better understand what AmplifyChange is looking for, how applications are assessed, and tips for preparing a strong proposal. Read More: https://amplifychange.org/application-guidance/ For more opportunities for youth-led organisations, NGOs, civil society groups, scholarships, fellowships, grants, jobs, and leadership programmes worldwide, keep visiting OFY and stay connected for the latest updates. The post AmplifyChange Strengthening Grants 2026: Up to £200,000 Available for SRHR Advocacy Projects in Eligible Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Commonwealth Students’ Association Opens Applications for Campus Ambassadors Across the Commonwealth 2026
summary: The Commonwealth Students’ Association has officially launched applications for its highly anticipated 2026 Campus Ambassador Programme, inviting passionate and impact-driven students from tertiary institutions across Commonwealth nations to become representatives of student voices within their campuses and communities. This prestigious opportunity is designed to strengthen grassroots student engagement, leadership development, advocacy participation, and institutional collaboration throughout the Commonwealth. Through this initiative, selected ambassadors will serve as a direct link between the Commonwealth Students’ Association (CSA) and students within their universities or colleges, helping to promote meaningful participation in student-centered programmes and campaigns. The CSA Campus Ambassador Programme offers students an opportunity to develop leadership skills, build international networks, participate in advocacy initiatives, and contribute to shaping the future of student representation across Commonwealth countries. About the Commonwealth Students’ Association Campus Ambassador Programme The CSA Campus Ambassador Programme is aimed at empowering students who are passionate about leadership, student engagement, advocacy, and community development. Selected ambassadors will become official student representatives of the Commonwealth Students’ Association within their institutions. Ambassadors will support the implementation of CSA initiatives on campus while mobilising fellow students to participate in activities, campaigns, and programmes that promote student empowerment and collaboration. The initiative also seeks to build stronger connections between student communities across different Commonwealth countries, encouraging knowledge sharing, dialogue, and youth participation in global conversations. Responsibilities of CSA Campus Ambassadors Students selected for the programme will play an active role in promoting the vision and activities of the Commonwealth Students’ Association within their institutions. Key responsibilities include: Promoting CSA programmes, initiatives, and campaigns within campus communities. Mobilising students to participate in leadership and advocacy activities. Supporting institutional engagement and grassroots mobilisation. Encouraging student participation in research and policy conversations. Acting as a communication bridge between CSA and students on campus. Assisting with awareness campaigns and student-focused events. Strengthening student representation and advocacy efforts within institutions. The role provides students with an opportunity to gain practical leadership experience while contributing meaningfully to their academic and social communities. Who Can Apply? The Commonwealth Students’ Association welcomes applications from students who meet the following eligibility criteria: Applicants must currently be enrolled in a recognised tertiary institution within a Commonwealth country. Applicants must be at least 18 years old. Applicants should be in good academic standing. Applicants must be able to dedicate a minimum of five hours per week to CSA activities. Applicants should demonstrate enthusiasm for advocacy, grassroots mobilisation, and student engagement. Applicants should possess strong interest in leadership and community impact. Importantly, prior student leadership experience is considered an advantage but is not mandatory. This means emerging student leaders with passion, commitment, and dedication are strongly encouraged to apply. Benefits of Becoming a CSA Campus Ambassador The programme provides ambassadors with access to a range of personal, professional, and leadership development opportunities. Benefits include: 1. Leadership Development Opportunities Ambassadors will gain practical leadership experience through campus engagement activities, advocacy initiatives, and programme coordination. 2. Capacity Building and Mentorship Participants will receive mentorship opportunities and access to learning experiences aimed at strengthening communication, leadership, and organisational skills. 3. International Networking The programme connects ambassadors with students, youth leaders, and institutions across Commonwealth countries, creating opportunities for collaboration and global networking. 4. Participation in CSA Initiatives Campus ambassadors will have opportunities to actively participate in various CSA programmes, campaigns, and advocacy activities. 5. Recognition and Certificates Outstanding ambassadors may receive Certificates of Service and recommendation letters in recognition of their commitment and contributions. Duration of Appointment The CSA Campus Ambassador appointment will run for an initial one-year term. However, ambassadors who demonstrate exceptional commitment and performance may have opportunities for renewal. Is the Programme Paid? The Commonwealth Students’ Association has clarified that the Campus Ambassador role is voluntary and non-remunerated. Despite this, the programme offers significant value for students seeking to build leadership experience, expand professional networks, and contribute to international student advocacy initiatives. Application Deadline Interested applicants must submit their applications before: 15 June 2026 (11:59 PM GMT) Students are encouraged to apply early to avoid last-minute issues during submission. Why Students Should Apply The CSA Campus Ambassador Programme represents a unique opportunity for students to become active contributors to student leadership and advocacy efforts within the Commonwealth. Beyond campus representation, ambassadors gain exposure to international networks, leadership development opportunities, and meaningful engagement in conversations that shape the future of education and student empowerment. Students who are passionate about leadership, youth development, advocacy, and community engagement are strongly encouraged to seize this opportunity and become part of a growing network of changemakers across the Commonwealth. Learn more and apply Interested students can apply through the official Commonwealth Students’ Association application portal HERE . Click HERE to learn more about the Commonwealth Students’ Association for Campus Ambassadors . Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here . The post Commonwealth Students’ Association Opens Applications for Campus Ambassadors Across the Commonwealth 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online Opportunity by IJSC
summary: Applications are now open for the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship offered through the Institute for Journalism and Social Change (IJSC) in partnership with the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme . This scholarship is designed for aspiring journalists, researchers, activists, storytellers, and social justice advocates who want to use investigative journalism and data-driven reporting to expose injustice, challenge harmful systems, and create real-world impact. It is especially focused on journalism that advances gender justice, protects women’s rights, defends LGBTQI rights, and amplifies voices from communities that have historically been underrepresented in mainstream media. The deadline to apply is 6pm GMT on 31st May 2026 . About the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship The Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship is an opportunity for students who want to study the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme while developing practical skills in investigative journalism, data journalism, feminist reporting, and cross-border storytelling. The scholarship is offered in partnership with the Institute for Journalism and Social Change , an organization that brings together journalists, activists, researchers, and social justice advocates to work on powerful investigations and collaborative projects. The scholarship is not only about funding a degree. It is also about supporting people who want to use journalism as a tool for justice. Successful applicants will gain access to training, mentorship, networking opportunities, and practical publishing experience. This makes the scholarship especially valuable for applicants who want to build careers in: Investigative journalism Data journalism Global journalism Human rights reporting Gender justice advocacy NGO communications Research and policy advocacy Social justice media Cross-border investigative projects Scholarship Value and Benefits The Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship offers significant academic, professional, and practical benefits. Successful scholarship recipients will receive: 1. Full Tuition Fee Coverage The scholarship covers 100% of tuition fees for the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme. The tuition fee value is £12,000 . This means selected students will be able to pursue the programme without paying tuition fees, reducing one of the biggest barriers to postgraduate study. 2. Access to IJSC Networking and Training Events Scholarship recipients will have opportunities to participate in IJSC networking and training events . These events can help students connect with: Experienced journalists Investigative reporters Researchers Activists Editors Media professionals Social justice organizations Potential collaborators For students who want to build careers in journalism or advocacy, these networks can be extremely valuable. 3. Mentorship and Career Advice Recipients will also receive mentorship and career guidance from IJSC associates and advisors . This mentorship can help students develop: Investigative story ideas Data journalism skills Reporting strategies Ethical journalism practices Career direction Publication opportunities Confidence in professional storytelling Mentorship is especially important in investigative journalism because strong investigations require not only technical skills but also editorial judgment, ethical awareness, persistence, and support. 4. Practical Projects for Publication Scholarship recipients will have the opportunity to work on practical projects for publication . This is one of the strongest parts of the scholarship because it allows students to move beyond theory and into real-world journalism practice. Previous IJSC investigations have received global media attention, including coverage by major outlets such as: Al Jazeera The Guardian Mail Guardian These investigations have also led to real-world impact. For example, in 2023, Norway, Denmark, and Sweden reportedly changed funding practices after IJSC revealed their support, through an intermediary, for anti-LGBT groups in Uganda, despite the donors’ rights commitments. This shows that IJSC’s work is not only academic or theoretical. It can influence public debate, institutional accountability, donor practices, and human rights outcomes. About the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online Programme The SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme is developed and delivered in partnership with the Institute for Journalism and Social Change. The programme focuses on journalism that is global, data-driven, socially aware, and rooted in justice. It is especially suitable for students who want to understand how journalism can expose injustice, challenge dominant narratives, and support marginalized communities. Because the programme is online, students can study remotely from wherever they are based. This makes it accessible to applicants who may not be able to relocate for postgraduate study. Key Programme Information Here are the main details applicants should know: Start Date The programme starts in September 2026 . Duration Students can study either: Full-time for 1 year , or Part-time for 2 years This flexibility makes the programme suitable for applicants who may be balancing study with work, family responsibilities, activism, freelance journalism, or other commitments. Location The programme is delivered online . This scholarship opportunity is specifically for students who will study remotely. Students will be able to work on practical cross-border projects while earning their degree from wherever they are based. Number of Scholarships Available There are two scholarships available . Because only two scholarships are available, applicants should take time to prepare a strong, thoughtful, and well-written application. Why This Scholarship Matters This scholarship matters because journalism is one of the most powerful tools for exposing injustice and holding institutions accountable. However, many communities most affected by injustice are often underrepresented in the media. Their stories may be ignored, misrepresented, or told without context. This scholarship seeks to support people who can bring lived experience, ethical reporting, and feminist analysis into investigative journalism. The opportunity is especially important for applicants interested in stories related to: Women’s rights LGBTQI rights Gender-based injustice Social inequality Global South perspectives Donor accountability Human rights Media representation Data-driven investigations Social justice reporting Why You Should Apply There are several strong reasons to apply for the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship. 1. You Will Learn Specialized Investigative and Data Journalism Skills The programme helps students learn how to tell powerful, evidence-based stories. You will learn how to use data, research, investigation, and storytelling to expose issues affecting women, LGBTQI communities, and other marginalized groups around the world. This is important because many modern investigations require more than interviews and traditional reporting. Journalists increasingly need to work with: Data sets Documents Cross-border evidence Public records Digital research tools Collaborative reporting methods Human rights frameworks 2. You Will Study Journalism Through Feminist and Decolonized Approaches The curriculum emphasizes inclusive and justice-oriented perspectives. This means the programme does not treat journalism as neutral in a shallow way. Instead, it recognizes that power, history, gender, race, geography, and colonial legacies shape how stories are told and whose voices are heard. A feminist and decolonized approach to journalism asks important questions such as: Who is being represented? Who is being ignored? Who benefits from the current narrative? Whose evidence is treated as credible? How can journalism avoid reproducing harm? How can stories be told with dignity and context? 3. You Can Study Online From Wherever You Are The programme is fully online, allowing students to study remotely. This is especially useful for applicants who cannot relocate due to cost, visa barriers, employment, family responsibilities, safety concerns, or community commitments. The online format makes the scholarship more accessible while still allowing students to participate in meaningful cross-border journalism projects. 4. You Can Turn Lived Experience Into Impactful Journalism This scholarship specifically values applicants who want to use their lived experience to inform their journalism. That does not mean applicants must only report on their personal experiences. Rather, it means the scholarship recognizes that people who have experienced or closely witnessed injustice can bring important insight, sensitivity, and urgency to their reporting. Lived experience can help journalists ask better questions, notice overlooked patterns, challenge stereotypes, and tell stories with greater depth. 5. The Programme Can Support Multiple Career Paths The SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme can prepare students for careers in several fields, including: Global journalism Investigative journalism Data journalism NGO work Human rights advocacy Policy research Campaign communications Social justice storytelling Media development Research-based advocacy This makes the scholarship valuable not only for people who want to work in newsrooms, but also for those who want to use journalism skills in civil society, advocacy, research, or international development. Eligibility Requirements Applicants must meet the academic and programme requirements. To be eligible, applicants should have: A minimum 2:2 UK undergraduate degree , or An international equivalent qualification Scholarship applicants must also separately apply to the SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online programme. Scholarship awards are conditional on gaining entry to the programme. This means that even if an applicant is selected for the scholarship, they must also be accepted into the MA programme before the scholarship can be confirmed. How to Apply To apply for the Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship, applicants must complete the online application form. The application form will ask applicants to: Paste in their CV. Answer three written questions. Each answer should be between 300 and 400 words . Application Questions Applicants will be asked to respond to three questions. 1. How would this scholarship help you apply your lived experience to impactful journalism? This question asks applicants to explain the connection between their background, lived experience, and their journalism goals. A strong answer should show: What experiences have shaped your perspective Why those experiences matter for journalism How you want to use your insight responsibly What kind of impact you hope to create Why this scholarship is important for your journey 2. What is your specific interest in investigative and data journalism for gender justice? This question focuses on your motivation and area of interest. A strong answer may discuss: Why investigative journalism matters to you Why data journalism is important for accountability What gender justice issues you care about How evidence-based reporting can expose injustice What kinds of stories you want to investigate 3. Give an example of a story that could have been told better with a feminist approach, and explain why. This question tests your ability to think critically about media coverage. A strong answer should identify a story and explain: How it was originally reported What was missing from the coverage Whose voices were ignored What assumptions shaped the story How a feminist approach would have improved it Why better reporting would have mattered Application Deadline The deadline to apply is 6pm GMT on 31st May 2026 . Applicants should prepare their materials early, especially because the application requires a CV and three written responses of 300 to 400 words each. Since there are only two scholarships available, applicants should avoid submitting rushed answers. Strong applications should be clear, specific, personal, and connected to the scholarship’s purpose. Final Thoughts The Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship is a powerful opportunity for people who want to use journalism to challenge injustice and create social change. With full tuition coverage worth £12,000, mentorship, training, networking, and opportunities to work on practical projects for publication, this scholarship offers more than financial support. It offers a pathway into meaningful, justice-focused journalism. For applicants passionate about gender justice, LGBTQI rights, Global South perspectives, data journalism, and investigative reporting, this opportunity could be a major step toward building a career that combines storytelling, evidence, activism, and public accountability. Applications close at 6pm GMT on 31st May 2026 . APPLY HERE. For more information about this opportunity click here. Discover more Scholarship opportunities on OFY here. The post Feminist Investigative Journalism Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded SOAS MA Global Data Journalism Online Opportunity by IJSC appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Malmö Residency 2026: A Month‑Long Creative Residency in Sweden’s Most Experimental City
summary: The Malmö Residency 2026 invites innovators, creators, researchers, entrepreneurs, and explorers from around the world to spend one month in Malmö—Sweden’s vibrant, youthful, and forward‑thinking coastal city. This city‑wide residency provides time, space, and community support to help you start, develop, or complete a project, idea, or exploration. Applications are open until June 3, 2026. This opportunity includes travel, accommodation, workspaces, community access, and deep immersion into Malmö’s creative and business ecosystems. About the Malmö Residency The Malmö business community collectively hosts this residency, offering participants a unique environment to focus on their ideas while connecting with local innovators. As stated in the document: “We – the business community of Malmö – invite you to spend time in the not-so-royal third-biggest city in Sweden.” The residency takes place in September/October 2026 , with a mandatory stay between September 9 and September 20 . Only five exclusive spots are available for the 2026 cohort. Priority Themes for 2026 For this third cohort, Malmö is especially interested in applicants excited by the following themes: Green Transition – Developing regenerative futures AI and Emerging Technologies – Elevating society, business, and creative work Food Innovation – Revolutionizing food production within city limits Cultural Creative Industries – Games, art, dance, music, and more Wise Digital Cities – Making cities transparent, democratic, and fun These themes reflect Malmö’s identity as a hub of experimentation and cross‑disciplinary collaboration. What’s Included in the Residency Participants receive a comprehensive package of support and resources, including: Travel to and from Malmö (preferably by train) One month of accommodation A bike for local mobility Access to workspaces across the city Advice from a broad community of experts Access to event venues, equipment, and production guidance Equipment and books to borrow Material libraries Industry‑grade testbeds Introductions to Malmö’s startup and game development communities Exhibitions at museums and art spaces Outdoor experiences, including beaches and allemansrätten Access to Malmö’s top‑ranked food and beverage scene The document emphasizes: “No strings attached really. We are a curious group and know that we benefit from an influx of open-minded people.” Why Malmö? Malmö is a coastal, diverse, and youthful city known for its openness and experimental culture. With 366,644 inhabitants , it is Sweden’s third‑largest and fastest‑growing major city. Nearly half the population is under 35 , and 187 nationalities are represented. Key highlights: A strong pay‑it‑forward culture 39,000 companies, mostly small and medium‑sized A thriving arts, design, and food scene Close proximity to Copenhagen Airport (20 minutes) 5 hours by train from Hamburg A balance of small‑city ease and big‑city ambition The Region and Academic Ecosystem The residency is embedded in the Öresund region—home to 4.2 million people and the largest labor market in the Nordics. Nearby institutions include: Malmö University – Strong in Interaction Design, Urban Studies, IoT, Data Society Lund University – One of the oldest universities in the world Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences (Alnarp) – Leading in food, agriculture, and landscape architecture World Maritime University – Global leader in maritime and ocean research This academic richness provides fertile ground for collaboration and inspiration. Events During the Residency Participants will be in Malmö during major international events, including: ECCV 2026 – European Conference on Computer Vision (Sept 8–12) The Drop – (Sept 15–16) EUMAS 2026 – (Sept 21–25) These events offer cutting‑edge knowledge and networking opportunities. Workspaces Available Participants will have access to Malmö’s most dynamic co‑working and innovation hubs: STPLN Media Evolution Minc Djäkne The Ground Form/Design Center Visa Information The residency cannot assist with visa applications . Applicants from visa‑required countries must secure their own documentation. How to Apply Deadline: June 3, 2026 Applications are open to global applicants who can commit to the mandatory residency dates. Apply here For more information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more opportunities here The post Malmö Residency 2026: A Month‑Long Creative Residency in Sweden’s Most Experimental City appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships 2026/2027: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship for At-Risk Scholars
summary: Loughborough University is offering the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships for students beginning their doctoral studies in October 2026, February 2027, or July 2027 . This scholarship has been created to support individuals whose education, safety, and life opportunities have been disrupted by conflict, persecution, displacement, or serious threats in their home countries. The Sanctuary Scholarship scheme reflects Loughborough University’s commitment to widening access to higher education for people who may otherwise be unable to pursue advanced study. For many scholars facing danger, forced displacement, or political and social instability, continuing education can become extremely difficult or impossible without targeted institutional support. This scholarship is designed to remove major financial barriers and help eligible candidates pursue a PhD in a safe and supportive academic environment. The scholarship covers 100% of tuition fees and provides a stipend of £21,805 per year , set at the recommended UKRI rate, for up to three years of full-time PhD study . The stipend is also subject to annual uplifts. Part-time PhD study in the UK may also be considered under specific conditions. The application deadline is Monday 22 June 2026 . About the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships The Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships are intended for individuals who can be described as at-risk scholars . These are people whose lives, education, or future opportunities have been seriously affected by dangerous or unstable circumstances such as conflict, persecution, war, displacement, or threats to personal safety. The scheme exists because Loughborough University recognises that talent and academic potential are not always matched by equal access to opportunity. Some highly capable students may be unable to progress to doctoral study because they have been forced to leave their home, live in unsafe conditions, or face persecution if they remain in or return to their country. Through this scholarship, the University aims to provide access to advanced research study for people who need meaningful institutional support to continue their academic journey. Scholarship Benefits Successful applicants for the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships will receive a generous support package for doctoral study. The scholarship provides: Full tuition fee coverage The scholarship covers 100% of tuition fees for the duration of the award. This removes one of the biggest financial barriers to undertaking a PhD at a UK university. Annual stipend Scholars will receive a stipend set at the recommended UKRI rate of £21,805 per year . Three years of support for full-time PhD study The award is available for a period of three years for full-time PhD study. Annual stipend uplifts The stipend is subject to annual uplifts, meaning the amount may increase in line with relevant updates. Part-time study may be considered Applicants who wish to study part-time in the UK may also be considered, but specific conditions apply. University-funded scholarship The scholarships are funded through Loughborough University funds. What the Scholarship Does Not Cover Although this is a highly supportive scholarship, applicants should note that it does not cover every possible cost connected to study in the UK. The scholarship does not cover: Travel costs Visa application costs Other related relocation costs, where required Students are expected to fund their own travel and visa costs through other sources. This is an important point for applicants to consider before applying, especially those who are currently outside the UK and may need to relocate in order to begin their PhD studies. Entry Dates for the Scholarship The Sanctuary PhD Scholarships are available for entry in: October 2026 February 2027 July 2027 Applicants should ensure that their PhD offer from Loughborough University aligns with one of these available entry points. Eligibility Criteria To be eligible for the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships, applicants must meet several important conditions. Applicants must: Hold an offer for a PhD programme at Loughborough University Applicants must already have received an offer of study for a postgraduate research programme at Loughborough University. This offer may be either: Conditional Unconditional Scholarship applications will not be considered unless the applicant is holding an offer at the time of submitting the scholarship application. Apply for a postgraduate research programme first Applicants cannot apply for the scholarship before applying for and receiving an offer from Loughborough University. The scholarship application must come after the PhD offer has been issued. Show evidence of exceptional academic achievement Applicants must demonstrate strong academic ability. However, the University considers academic achievement in relation to the applicant’s circumstances. This is important because many at-risk scholars may have experienced serious disruption to their education. The University is not only looking at achievement in ideal conditions. It is also considering achievement relative to the barriers, instability, or disruption the applicant has faced. Be considered an at-risk scholar The scholarship is designed specifically for people whose safety, education, or life chances have been affected by conflict, persecution, displacement, or similar circumstances. Who Is Considered an At-Risk Scholar? Loughborough University explains that it is difficult to provide one fixed definition of an at-risk scholar . However, the University uses a framework to assess whether an applicant’s circumstances meet the purpose of the Sanctuary Scholarship scheme. Applicants may be considered at risk if their situation involves one or more of the following: Living in a region affected by conflict or war Applicants may qualify if their home is located in a region where conflict or war places their life, or the lives of their family members, in danger. Risk of forced displacement Applicants may be considered at risk if they could be forcibly displaced due to persecution or dangerous conditions. Risk of imprisonment, injury, death, or persecution Applicants may qualify if they or their family face imminent danger, including the risk of imprisonment, injury, death, or persecution. Danger if required to return home Applicants who are currently in the UK may also qualify if returning to their home country would place their life or safety at much greater risk. This framework allows the University to assess applicants whose circumstances may vary widely but who share a common need for protection, support, and access to education. How to Apply for the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships The scholarship application process is an open competition . This means eligible applicants will be assessed competitively, and awards will be made based on the University’s selection process. Applicants must follow the correct order when applying. Step 1: Apply for a PhD programme at Loughborough University Before applying for the Sanctuary Scholarship, applicants must first apply for a postgraduate research programme at Loughborough University. Step 2: Receive a conditional or unconditional offer Applicants must wait until they have received an official offer letter from Loughborough University. This offer can be conditional or unconditional. Step 3: Apply for the scholarship Once an applicant has received an offer, they can apply for the Sanctuary Scholarship using the online application form provided by the University. The application link was referenced as “Apply now” in the provided information, but no direct URL was included here. Step 4: Complete all parts of the application form Applicants must ensure that every part of the scholarship application form is completed in full. Incomplete applications may not be considered. Step 5: Wait for confirmation and outcome After submitting the application, applicants should receive a confirmation email stating that the application has been received. All applicants will be notified of the outcome by email. Selection Process Applications will first be shortlisted. After the shortlisting stage, the final decision on the scholarship awards will be made by a selection panel at Loughborough University. If an applicant is shortlisted, the University will provide further information about the next steps in the process. Applicants who receive an award while holding a conditional PhD offer should note that the scholarship will only be confirmed if they meet all conditions attached to their offer. Important Notes for Applicants Applicants should carefully read the important conditions attached to the scholarship before submitting their application. Key points include: You must already hold a PhD offer Scholarship applications will not be considered unless the applicant is already holding a conditional or unconditional offer from Loughborough University. Do not apply before receiving your offer letter Applicants should not submit a scholarship application until they have received their offer letter. Incomplete applications may not be considered All sections of the application form must be completed in full. Late applications will not be accepted Applications received after the closing date will not be considered. Conditional offers must be fully met If a scholarship is awarded to an applicant with a conditional offer, the award will depend on the applicant meeting those conditions in full. Travel and visa costs are not covered Applicants must make separate arrangements to cover travel and visa-related expenses where required. Part-Time PhD Study Conditions The scholarship may consider applicants who wish to study part-time in the UK, but there are important restrictions. If an applicant is currently outside the UK and wishes to study part-time, Loughborough University will not be able to sponsor them in the UK on a student visa. For applicants already based in the UK who wish to be considered for a part-time PhD scholarship, additional conditions apply. Applicants in full-time employment will not be eligible for a part-time scholarship. However, applicants may be considered if they: Work part-time Have caring responsibilities Have other requirements that make part-time study necessary This means part-time awards are possible, but they are not automatic and will depend on the applicant’s personal circumstances. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships is: Monday 22 June 2026 Applications submitted after this date will not be considered. Why This Scholarship Matters The Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships provide more than financial support. They offer an opportunity for at-risk scholars to continue their education, rebuild academic pathways, and contribute to research despite the serious challenges they may have faced. For individuals whose education has been interrupted by conflict, persecution, or displacement, access to doctoral study can be life-changing. A PhD can open doors to academic careers, research leadership, policy influence, professional advancement, and long-term personal stability. The scholarship also strengthens the role of universities as institutions of protection, opportunity, and intellectual freedom. By supporting at-risk scholars, Loughborough University helps preserve academic talent that might otherwise be lost due to circumstances beyond the applicant’s control. Final Reminder The Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships are open to eligible PhD applicants who already hold an offer from Loughborough University and who can demonstrate that they are at risk due to conflict, persecution, displacement, or serious threats to safety. Applicants should first secure a PhD offer, prepare a complete scholarship application, and submit it before the deadline of Monday 22 June 2026 . APPLY HERE . For more information about this opportunity click here. Discover more scholarship opportunities on OFY here. The post Loughborough University Sanctuary PhD Scholarships 2026/2027: Fully Funded PhD Scholarship for At-Risk Scholars appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NASA Fall 2026 Science Communications Internship at Goddard Space Flight Center: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring Communicators
summary: Introduction The NASA Fall 2026 Science Communications Internship at the Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC) in Greenbelt, Maryland, offers an extraordinary opportunity for undergraduate and graduate students to gain hands-on experience in multimedia storytelling, public engagement, and internal communications. This in-person internship is designed for students who are passionate about science communication, journalism, media production, public relations, design, and related fields. With access to NASA’s most groundbreaking missions and communication teams, interns will immerse themselves in real-world projects that shape how the world understands space, Earth science, and technological innovation. This article provides a detailed overview of the internship, including responsibilities, focus areas, eligibility, application tips, and key dates. It is crafted to help students understand the full scope of the opportunity and prepare a strong application. About the NASA Science Communications Internship NASA internships are immersive educational experiences that allow students to participate in NASA-related research, operations, and communications. This particular internship focuses on multimedia, social media, public engagement, and internal communications within NASA’s bustling communications department. Interns will collaborate with expert writers, multimedia producers, public affairs officers, and experience designers to create compelling content that highlights NASA’s most exciting missions. These include: The Hubble Space Telescope The James Webb Space Telescope The Roman Space Telescope Artemis lunar exploration missions Earth observation satellites Solar observation missions Interns will help translate complex scientific discoveries into engaging stories for diverse audiences. Internship Focus Areas This posting covers two primary roles. Applicants may indicate a preference for one or both. 1. Multimedia Newsroom Interns in this track will contribute to high-impact communication campaigns by creating: Web features Videos Media interviews Social media content Livestreams Digital storytelling assets Students may specialize in writing, video production, or digital/social media. 2. Public Engagement This track focuses on outreach and internal engagement, including: Public outreach campaigns Event planning Tour organization Workforce engagement Graphic and display design Photography Interns may choose to focus on tours, graphics, photography, or internal communications. Key Responsibilities Interns will engage in a wide range of creative and strategic tasks, including: Writing Designing Hosting Engaging with audiences Leading and collaborating Teaching and presenting Creating multimedia content Researching and analyzing Captioning and shooting media Updating digital platforms Networking and managing communications Tracking and monitoring engagement Building and blending creative assets Exploring new storytelling approaches Desired Attributes NASA seeks students who demonstrate: Unbounded curiosity A creative spirit A passion for storytelling Interest or experience in science Strong communication skills Ability to work collaboratively Initiative and adaptability Some of NASA’s most successful interns have been scientists with communication skills or communicators with a strong interest in science. Work Schedule and Format Full-time commitment: Approximately 40 hours per week Schedule: Monday through Friday, regular working hours Flexibility: Minor adjustments for mid-day classes may be possible Format: In-person at Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC), Greenbelt, Maryland NASA emphasizes that the internship is best performed on-site due to the hands-on nature of the work. Application Tips To strengthen your application, NASA recommends: Including a personal statement outlining your internship goals Highlighting relevant coursework, internships, and work experience Providing links to work samples (writing, video, design, photography, etc.) Explaining how the internship aligns with your long-term career aspirations Key Dates Registration Start Date: March 16, 2026 Registration End Date: May 22, 2026 Internship Session: Fall 2026 Applicants must submit all materials before the registration deadline. Location and Eligibility Requirements Location: Goddard Space Flight Center (GSFC), Greenbelt, Maryland Citizenship Requirement: U.S. citizens only Eligible Academic Levels: Undergraduate Freshman Undergraduate Sophomore Undergraduate Junior Undergraduate Senior Graduate Master’s students Preferred Majors NASA welcomes students from a wide range of communication, media, and design-related fields, including: Journalism Communications Media studies Public relations Advertising Strategic communications Marketing Graphic design Photography Audiovisual communications Digital communication Publishing Radio, television, and digital media Visual and performing arts To apply and get more information about this opportunity visit here Discover more opportunities here The post NASA Fall 2026 Science Communications Internship at Goddard Space Flight Center: A Comprehensive Guide for Aspiring Communicators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: AfricaNenda SIIPS 2026 Report Cover Photo Competition: Win USD 500 for Your African Visual Storytelling
summary: The AfricaNenda Foundation has officially opened submissions for creatives, photographers, and visual artists to contribute the official cover design for the highly anticipated State of Inclusive Instant Payment Systems in Africa (SIIPS) 2026 Report. This exciting Request for Proposals (RFP) presents a unique opportunity for African visual storytellers to showcase their creativity, celebrate Africa’s diversity, and gain international visibility while competing for a USD 500 prize. The selected cover image or concept will become the visual identity of the SIIPS 2026 Report, a publication that highlights the progress, innovation, and future of inclusive instant payment systems across Africa. The initiative aims to visually communicate Africa’s digital transformation, entrepreneurship ecosystem, financial inclusion efforts, and regional connectivity through authentic African imagery. Applicants are encouraged to review previous SIIPS reports to better understand the storytelling style and visual direction expected for the 2026 edition. About the SIIPS 2026 Report Cover Opportunity The SIIPS report is recognized as an important publication documenting Africa’s evolving instant payment systems landscape. By contributing to the official report cover, photographers and creatives have an opportunity to position their work before policymakers, financial institutions, development organizations, fintech stakeholders, and audiences across Africa and beyond. AfricaNenda is specifically looking for visually compelling and professional concepts that reflect: Africa’s map and continental identity Authentic African faces and communities Africa’s cultural richness and regional diversity Innovation and financial inclusion Digital transactions and connectivity Trade and entrepreneurship Movement, networks, and human connection A modern and professional aesthetic suitable for a continental report The organization welcomes creative interpretations that combine storytelling, visual symbolism, and originality while remaining aligned with the report’s professional and policy-oriented audience. Prize for the Selected Cover Design The selected submission will receive: USD 500 cash prize Recognition through inclusion in the SIIPS 2026 Report Exposure to international development and financial inclusion stakeholders An opportunity to contribute to a major pan-African publication Selected contributors will later be required to submit original high-resolution non-watermarked files and sign a simple licensing agreement before payment is processed. Submission Requirements for Applicants Applicants must carefully follow all submission instructions to ensure eligibility. What to Include in Your Submission ZIP Folder All submission materials must be compiled into a single ZIP folder containing: Watermarked preview images in JPEG or PNG format Original photographs only A submission sample document in text, Word, or PDF format containing: Proposed cover Photographer’s full name Country where each image was taken Image captions or descriptions where possible Contact information including: Email address Phone number or WhatsApp number with country code Important Rules and Conditions AfricaNenda has outlined strict originality and ownership requirements for all entries. Key Conditions Include: AI-generated images are strictly prohibited All submissions must consist of original photographs taken by the entrant Submitted images must not have previously won or been accepted in photography competitions Photographers must own full rights to all submitted work Images featuring identifiable individuals must include signed consent or release forms Failure to meet any of these conditions may lead to disqualification. Why This Opportunity Matters for African Creatives This competition is more than just a photography submission call. It is an opportunity to visually shape conversations around financial inclusion and Africa’s digital economy. The SIIPS report reaches a broad network of: Financial institutions Policymakers Fintech companies Development organizations Investors Researchers Technology and payment ecosystem stakeholders Having your work featured on the report cover can significantly boost professional visibility and strengthen your creative portfolio. Deadline for Applications The deadline for submissions is: 15 June 2026 Applicants are encouraged to submit early to avoid technical issues or last-minute delays. How to Submit Your Entry All submissions must be sent via email to: communications@africanenda.org Applicants are advised to carefully review all requirements before submission and ensure that all materials are complete, professional, and properly organized. The SIIPS 2026 cover competition offers African creatives a rare opportunity to merge artistic storytelling with Africa’s digital transformation narrative while gaining continental recognition for their work. For more information about this opportunity visit here. Discover more competition opportunities here. The post AfricaNenda SIIPS 2026 Report Cover Photo Competition: Win USD 500 for Your African Visual Storytelling appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
